steps: A 4% annual increase means each year's population is $100\% + 4\% = 104\%$ of the prior year, so the multiplier is $1.04$.
Starting from 5,000 in 2008, after $x$ years the population is $p = 5{,}000 \cdot (1.04)^x$.

• Choice A — '$p=0.96(5{,}000)^x$': $0.96$ is a decay factor and $(5{,}000)^x$ is not a valid exponential growth form. ✗
  • Choice B — '$p=1.04(5{,}000)^x$': Has $5{,}000$ as the base raised to $x$, which grows absurdly fast; the initial value is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — '$p=5{,}000(0.96)^x$': Uses $0.96$, a decay multiplier (4% decrease, not increase). ✗
  • Choice D — '$p=5{,}000(1.04)^x$': Initial population 5,000 multiplied by growth factor $1.04$ each year for $x$ years. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
